<uploaded_files>

I've uploaded a python code repository in the directory /testbed. Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
Batch publish - support isolated reads
### Observed behavior

We currently don't support isolated reads for batch publish, meaning once you batch publish 10 messages any readers through message GET requests or consumers will observe these messages as they are applied sequentially and not as one atomic action. Due to that a reader could observe parts of the batch's effects, even if the full batch was committed.

Likely this is already an issue when using `Nats-Rollup`, since the rollup happens separately after persisting the message that contains the rollup. Which would mean you could observe the full stream including the rollup message in a read, even if the rollup should have purged data and not allowed you to see the previous messages within the same read.

### Expected behavior

Support isolated reads in the server, guaranteeing that readers are also "aware" of the batch semantics and either observe none of a batch's effects, or observe it in its entirety after commit.

### Server and client version

`main`

### Host environment

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

</pr_description>

3. Edit the sourcecode of the repo to resolve the issue

Your thinking should be thorough and so it's fine if it's very long.

  6101	        // event on exit in case there was an error (if message was not proposed).
  6102	        // Otherwise, the event will be sent from processJetStreamMsg when
  6103	        // invoked by the leader (from applyStreamEntries).

  6105	                defer func() {
  6106	                        if retErr != nil {
  6107	                                mt.sendEventFromJetStream(retErr)
  6108	                        }
  6109	                }()
  6110	        }

  6129	                        b, _ := json.Marshal(&JSPubAckResponse{PubAck: &PubAck{Stream: name}, Error: NewJSInsufficientResourcesError()})
  6130	                        outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, nil, b, nil, 0))
  6131	                }

  6133	                if node := mset.raftNode(); node != nil {
  6134	                        node.StepDown()
  6135	                }
  6136	                return NewJSInsufficientResourcesError()
  6137	        }
  6138	
  6139	        // Check here pre-emptively if we have exceeded our account limits.
  6140	        if exceeded, err := jsa.wouldExceedLimits(st, tierName, r, subject, hdr, msg); exceeded {
  6141	                if err == nil {
  6142	                        err = NewJSAccountResourcesExceededError()

  6430	                if err := node.ProposeMulti(entries); err == nil {
  6431	                        mset.trackReplicationTraffic(node, sz, r)
  6432	                } else {
  6433	                        // TODO(mvv): reset in-memory expected header maps
  6434	                        mset.clseq -= batchSeq
  6435	                }
  6436	
  6437	                // Check to see if we are being overrun.
  6438	                // TODO(dlc) - Make this a limit where we drop messages to protect ourselves, but allow to be configured.
  6439	                if mset.clseq-(lseq+mset.clfs) > streamLagWarnThreshold {
  6440	                        lerr := fmt.Errorf("JetStream stream '%s > %s' has high message lag", jsa.acc().Name, name)
  6441	                        s.RateLimitWarnf("%s", lerr.Error())
  6442	                }
  6443	        }
  6444	        mset.clMu.Unlock()
  6445	        b.cleanupLocked(batchId, batches)
  6446	        batches.mu.Unlock()
  6447	        return nil
  6448	}
  6449	
  6450	// Used to signal inbound message to registered consumers.

    30	// StorageType determines how messages are stored for retention.
    31	type StorageType int
    32	
    33	const (
    34	        // File specifies on disk, designated by the JetStream config StoreDir.
    35	        FileStorage = StorageType(22)
    36	        // MemoryStorage specifies in memory only.
    37	        MemoryStorage = StorageType(33)
    38	)

    40	var (
    41	        // ErrStoreClosed is returned when the store has been closed
    42	        ErrStoreClosed = errors.New("store is closed")
    43	        // ErrStoreMsgNotFound when message was not found but was expected to be.
    44	        ErrStoreMsgNotFound = errors.New("no message found")
    45	        // ErrStoreEOF is returned when message seq is greater than the last sequence.
    46	        ErrStoreEOF = errors.New("stream store EOF")
    47	        // ErrMaxMsgs is returned when we have discard new as a policy and we reached the message limit.
    48	        ErrMaxMsgs = errors.New("maximum messages exceeded")
    49	        // ErrMaxBytes is returned when we have discard new as a policy and we reached the bytes limit.
    50	        ErrMaxBytes = errors.New("maximum bytes exceeded")
    51	        // ErrMaxMsgsPerSubject is returned when we have discard new as a policy and we reached the message limit per subject.
    52	        ErrMaxMsgsPerSubject = errors.New("maximum messages per subject exceeded")
    53	        // ErrStoreSnapshotInProgress is returned when RemoveMsg or EraseMsg is called
    54	        // while a snapshot is in progress.
    55	        ErrStoreSnapshotInProgress = errors.New("snapshot in progress")
    56	        // ErrMsgTooLarge is returned when a message is considered too large.
    57	        ErrMsgTooLarge = errors.New("message too large")
    58	        // ErrStoreWrongType is for when you access the wrong storage type.
    59	        ErrStoreWrongType = errors.New("wrong storage type")
    60	        // ErrNoAckPolicy is returned when trying to update a consumer's acks with no ack policy.
    61	        ErrNoAckPolicy = errors.New("ack policy is none")
    62	        // ErrSequenceMismatch is returned when storing a raw message and the expected sequence is wrong.
    63	        ErrSequenceMismatch = errors.New("expected sequence does not match store")
    64	        // ErrCorruptStreamState
    65	        ErrCorruptStreamState = errors.New("stream state snapshot is corrupt")
    66	        // ErrTooManyResults
    67	        ErrTooManyResults = errors.New("too many matching results for request")
    68	)

   136	// RetentionPolicy determines how messages in a set are retained.
   137	type RetentionPolicy int
   138	
   139	const (
   140	        // LimitsPolicy (default) means that messages are retained until any given limit is reached.
   141	        // This could be one of MaxMsgs, MaxBytes, or MaxAge.
   142	        LimitsPolicy RetentionPolicy = iota
   143	        // InterestPolicy specifies that when all known consumers have acknowledged a message it can be removed.
   144	        InterestPolicy
   145	        // WorkQueuePolicy specifies that when the first worker or subscriber acknowledges the message it can be removed.
   146	        WorkQueuePolicy
   147	)
   148	
   149	// Discard Policy determines how we proceed when limits of messages or bytes are hit. The default, DicscardOld will
   150	// remove older messages. DiscardNew will fail to store the new message.
   151	type DiscardPolicy int
   152	
   153	const (
   154	        // DiscardOld will remove older messages to return to the limits.
   155	        DiscardOld = iota
   156	        // DiscardNew will error on a StoreMsg call
   157	        DiscardNew
   158	)
   159	
   160	// StreamState is information about the given stream.

   194	// SnapshotResult contains information about the snapshot.
   195	type SnapshotResult struct {
   196	        Reader io.ReadCloser
   197	        State  StreamState
   198	        errCh  chan string
   199	}
   200	
   201	const (
   202	        // Magic is used to identify stream state encodings.
   203	        streamStateMagic = uint8(42)
   204	        // Version
   205	        streamStateVersion = uint8(1)
   206	        // Magic / Identifier for run length encodings.
   207	        runLengthMagic = uint8(33)
   208	        // Magic / Identifier for AVL seqsets.
   209	        seqSetMagic = uint8(22)
   210	)
   211	
   212	// Interface for DeleteBlock.
   213	// These will be of three types:
   214	// 1. AVL seqsets.
   215	// 2. Run length encoding of a deleted range.
   216	// 3. Legacy []uint64
   217	type DeleteBlock interface {
   218	        State() (first, last, num uint64)
   219	        Range(f func(uint64) bool)
   220	}
   221	
   222	type DeleteBlocks []DeleteBlock
   223	
   224	// StreamReplicatedState represents what is encoded in a binary stream snapshot used
   225	// for stream replication in an NRG.
   226	type StreamReplicatedState struct {
   227	        Msgs     uint64
   228	        Bytes    uint64
   229	        FirstSeq uint64
   230	        LastSeq  uint64
   231	        Failed   uint64
   232	        Deleted  DeleteBlocks
   233	}
   234	
   235	// Determine if this is an encoded stream state.
   236	func IsEncodedStreamState(buf []byte) bool {
   237	        return len(buf) >= hdrLen && buf[0] == streamStateMagic && buf[1] == streamStateVersion
   238	}
   239	
   240	var ErrBadStreamStateEncoding = errors.New("bad stream state encoding")
   241	
   242	func DecodeStreamState(buf []byte) (*StreamReplicatedState, error) {
   243	        ss := &StreamReplicatedState{}
   244	        if len(buf) < hdrLen || buf[0] != streamStateMagic || buf[1] != streamStateVersion {
   245	                return nil, ErrBadStreamStateEncoding
   246	        }
   247	        var bi = hdrLen
   248	
   249	        readU64 := func() uint64 {
   250	                if bi < 0 || bi >= len(buf) {
   251	                        bi = -1
   252	                        return 0
   253	                }
   254	                num, n := binary.Uvarint(buf[bi:])
   255	                if n <= 0 {
   256	                        bi = -1
   257	                        return 0
   258	                }
   259	                bi += n
   260	                return num
   261	        }
   262	
   263	        parserFailed := func() bool {
   264	                return bi < 0
   265	        }
   266	
   267	        ss.Msgs = readU64()
   268	        ss.Bytes = readU64()
   269	        ss.FirstSeq = readU64()
   270	        ss.LastSeq = readU64()
   271	        ss.Failed = readU64()
   272	
   273	        if parserFailed() {
   274	                return nil, ErrCorruptStreamState
   275	        }
   276	
   277	        if numDeleted := readU64(); numDeleted > 0 {
   278	                // If we have some deleted blocks.
   279	                for l := len(buf); l > bi; {
   280	                        switch buf[bi] {
   281	                        case seqSetMagic:
   282	                                dmap, n, err := avl.Decode(buf[bi:])
   283	                                if err != nil {
   284	                                        return nil, ErrCorruptStreamState
   285	                                }
   286	                                bi += n
   287	                                ss.Deleted = append(ss.Deleted, dmap)
   288	                        case runLengthMagic:
   289	                                bi++
   290	                                var rl DeleteRange
   291	                                rl.First = readU64()
   292	                                rl.Num = readU64()
   293	                                if parserFailed() {
   294	                                        return nil, ErrCorruptStreamState
   295	                                }
   296	                                ss.Deleted = append(ss.Deleted, &rl)
   297	                        default:
   298	                                return nil, ErrCorruptStreamState
   299	                        }
   300	                }
   301	        }
   302	
   303	        return ss, nil
   304	}
   305	
   306	// DeleteRange is a run length encoded delete range.
   307	type DeleteRange struct {
   308	        First uint64
   309	        Num   uint64
   310	}
   311	
   312	func (dr *DeleteRange) State() (first, last, num uint64) {
   313	        deletesAfterFirst := dr.Num
   314	        if deletesAfterFirst > 0 {
   315	                deletesAfterFirst--
   316	        }
   317	        return dr.First, dr.First + deletesAfterFirst, dr.Num
   318	}
   319	
   320	// Range will range over all the deleted sequences represented by this block.
   321	func (dr *DeleteRange) Range(f func(uint64) bool) {
   322	        for seq := dr.First; seq < dr.First+dr.Num; seq++ {
   323	                if !f(seq) {
   324	                        return
   325	                }
   326	        }
   327	}
   328	
   329	// Legacy []uint64
   330	type DeleteSlice []uint64
   331	
   332	func (ds DeleteSlice) State() (first, last, num uint64) {
   333	        if len(ds) == 0 {
   334	                return 0, 0, 0
   335	        }
   336	        return ds[0], ds[len(ds)-1], uint64(len(ds))
   337	}
   338	
   339	// Range will range over all the deleted sequences represented by this []uint64.
   340	func (ds DeleteSlice) Range(f func(uint64) bool) {
   341	        for _, seq := range ds {
   342	                if !f(seq) {
   343	                        return
   344	                }
   345	        }
   346	}
   347	
   348	func (dbs DeleteBlocks) NumDeleted() (total uint64) {
   349	        for _, db := range dbs {
   350	                _, _, num := db.State()
   351	                total += num
   352	        }
   353	        return total
   354	}
   355	
   356	// ConsumerStore stores state on consumers for streams.
   357	type ConsumerStore interface {
   358	        SetStarting(sseq uint64) error
   359	        UpdateStarting(sseq uint64)
   360	        HasState() bool
   361	        UpdateDelivered(dseq, sseq, dc uint64, ts int64) error
   362	        UpdateAcks(dseq, sseq uint64) error
   363	        UpdateConfig(cfg *ConsumerConfig) error
   364	        Update(*ConsumerState) error
   365	        State() (*ConsumerState, error)
   366	        BorrowState() (*ConsumerState, error)
   367	        EncodedState() ([]byte, error)
   368	        Type() StorageType
   369	        Stop() error
   370	        Delete() error
   371	        StreamDelete() error
   372	}
   373	
   374	// SequencePair has both the consumer and the stream sequence. They point to same message.
   375	type SequencePair struct {
   376	        Consumer uint64 `json:"consumer_seq"`
   377	        Stream   uint64 `json:"stream_seq"`
   378	}
   379	
   380	// ConsumerState represents a stored state for a consumer.
   381	type ConsumerState struct {
   382	        // Delivered keeps track of last delivered sequence numbers for both the stream and the consumer.
   383	        Delivered SequencePair `json:"delivered"`
   384	        // AckFloor keeps track of the ack floors for both the stream and the consumer.
   385	        AckFloor SequencePair `json:"ack_floor"`
   386	        // These are both in stream sequence context.
   387	        // Pending is for all messages pending and the timestamp for the delivered time.
   388	        // This will only be present when the AckPolicy is ExplicitAck.
   389	        Pending map[uint64]*Pending `json:"pending,omitempty"`
   390	        // This is for messages that have been redelivered, so count > 1.
   391	        Redelivered map[uint64]uint64 `json:"redelivered,omitempty"`
   392	}
   393	
   394	// Encode consumer state.
   395	func encodeConsumerState(state *ConsumerState) []byte {
   396	        var hdr [seqsHdrSize]byte
   397	        var buf []byte
   398	
   399	        maxSize := seqsHdrSize
   400	        if lp := len(state.Pending); lp > 0 {
   401	                maxSize += lp*(3*binary.MaxVarintLen64) + binary.MaxVarintLen64
   402	        }
   403	        if lr := len(state.Redelivered); lr > 0 {
   404	                maxSize += lr*(2*binary.MaxVarintLen64) + binary.MaxVarintLen64
   405	        }
   406	        if maxSize == seqsHdrSize {
   407	                buf = hdr[:seqsHdrSize]
   408	        } else {
   409	                buf = make([]byte, maxSize)
   410	        }
   411	
   412	        // Write header
   413	        buf[0] = magic
   414	        buf[1] = 2
   415	
   416	        n := hdrLen
   417	        n += binary.PutUvarint(buf[n:], state.AckFloor.Consumer)
   418	        n += binary.PutUvarint(buf[n:], state.AckFloor.Stream)
   419	        n += binary.PutUvarint(buf[n:], state.Delivered.Consumer)
   420	        n += binary.PutUvarint(buf[n:], state.Delivered.Stream)
   421	        n += binary.PutUvarint(buf[n:], uint64(len(state.Pending)))
   422	
   423	        asflr := state.AckFloor.Stream
   424	        adflr := state.AckFloor.Consumer
   425	
   426	        // These are optional, but always write len. This is to avoid a truncate inline.
   427	        if len(state.Pending) > 0 {
   428	                // To save space we will use now rounded to seconds to be our base timestamp.
   429	                mints := time.Now().Round(time.Second).Unix()
   430	                // Write minimum timestamp we found from above.
   431	                n += binary.PutVarint(buf[n:], mints)
   432	
   433	                for k, v := range state.Pending {
   434	                        n += binary.PutUvarint(buf[n:], k-asflr)
   435	                        n += binary.PutUvarint(buf[n:], v.Sequence-adflr)
   436	                        // Downsample to seconds to save on space.
   437	                        // Subsecond resolution not needed for recovery etc.
   438	                        ts := v.Timestamp / int64(time.Second)
   439	                        n += binary.PutVarint(buf[n:], mints-ts)
   440	                }
   441	        }
   442	
   443	        // We always write the redelivered len.
   444	        n += binary.PutUvarint(buf[n:], uint64(len(state.Redelivered)))
   445	
   446	        // We expect these to be small.
   447	        if len(state.Redelivered) > 0 {
   448	                for k, v := range state.Redelivered {
   449	                        n += binary.PutUvarint(buf[n:], k-asflr)
   450	                        n += binary.PutUvarint(buf[n:], v)
   451	                }
   452	        }
   453	
   454	        return buf[:n]
   455	}
   456	
   457	// Represents a pending message for explicit ack or ack all.
   458	// Sequence is the original consumer sequence.
   459	type Pending struct {
   460	        Sequence  uint64
   461	        Timestamp int64
   462	}
   463	
   464	// TemplateStore stores templates.
   465	// Deprecated: stream templates are deprecated and will be removed in a future version.
   466	type TemplateStore interface {
   467	        Store(*streamTemplate) error
   468	        Delete(*streamTemplate) error
   469	}
   470	
   471	const (
   472	        limitsPolicyJSONString    = `"limits"`
   473	        interestPolicyJSONString  = `"interest"`
   474	        workQueuePolicyJSONString = `"workqueue"`
   475	)
   476	
   477	var (
   478	        limitsPolicyJSONBytes    = []byte(limitsPolicyJSONString)
   479	        interestPolicyJSONBytes  = []byte(interestPolicyJSONString)
   480	        workQueuePolicyJSONBytes = []byte(workQueuePolicyJSONString)
   481	)
   482	
   483	func (rp RetentionPolicy) String() string {
   484	        switch rp {
   485	        case LimitsPolicy:
   486	                return "Limits"
   487	        case InterestPolicy:
   488	                return "Interest"
   489	        case WorkQueuePolicy:
   490	                return "WorkQueue"
   491	        default:
   492	                return "Unknown Retention Policy"
   493	        }
   494	}
   495	
   496	func (rp RetentionPolicy) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
   497	        switch rp {
   498	        case LimitsPolicy:
   499	                return limitsPolicyJSONBytes, nil
   500	        case InterestPolicy:
   501	                return interestPolicyJSONBytes, nil
   502	        case WorkQueuePolicy:
   503	                return workQueuePolicyJSONBytes, nil
   504	        default:
   505	                return<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

    41	// JetStreamConfig determines this server's configuration.
    42	// MaxMemory and MaxStore are in bytes.
    43	type JetStreamConfig struct {
    44	        MaxMemory    int64         `json:"max_memory"`              // MaxMemory is the maximum size of memory type streams
    45	        MaxStore     int64         `json:"max_storage"`             // MaxStore is the maximum size of file store type streams
    46	        StoreDir     string        `json:"store_dir,omitempty"`     // StoreDir is where storage files are stored
    47	        SyncInterval time.Duration `json:"sync_interval,omitempty"` // SyncInterval is how frequently we sync to disk in the background by calling fsync
    48	        SyncAlways   bool          `json:"sync_always,omitempty"`   // SyncAlways indicates flushes are done after every write
    49	        Domain       string        `json:"domain,omitempty"`        // Domain is the JetStream domain
    50	        CompressOK   bool          `json:"compress_ok,omitempty"`   // CompressOK indicates if compression is supported
    51	        UniqueTag    string        `json:"unique_tag,omitempty"`    // UniqueTag is the unique tag assigned to this instance
    52	        Strict       bool          `json:"strict,omitempty"`        // Strict indicates if strict JSON parsing is performed
    53	}

   147	// This represents a jetstream enabled account.
   148	// Worth noting that we include the jetstream pointer, this is because
   149	// in general we want to be very efficient when receiving messages on
   150	// an internal sub for a stream, so we will direct link to the stream
   151	// and walk backwards as needed vs multiple hash lookups and locks, etc.
   152	type jsAccount struct {

   161	
   162	        // From server

   164	
   165	        // For limiting only running one checkAndSync at a time.

   167	
   168	        // Usage/limits related fields that will be protected by usageMu

   174	        apiErrors  uint64

   179	        lupdate    time.Time

   181	}

  7195	func (mset *stream) state() StreamState {
  7196	        return mset.stateWithDetail(false)
  7197	}
  7198	
  7199	func (mset *stream) stateWithDetail(details bool) StreamState {
  7200	        // mset.store does not change once set, so ok to reference here directly.
  7201	        // We do this elsewhere as well.
  7202	        store := mset.store
  7203	        if store == nil {
  7204	                return StreamState{}
  7205	        }
  7206	
  7207	        // Currently rely on store for details.
  7208	        if details {
  7209	                return store.State()
  7210	        }
  7211	        // Here we do the fast version.
  7212	        var state StreamState
  7213	        store.FastState(&state)
  7214	        return state
  7215	}

    41	// StreamConfigRequest is used to create or update a stream.
    42	type StreamConfigRequest struct {
    43	        StreamConfig
    44	        // This is not part of the StreamConfig, because its scoped to request,
    45	        // and not to the stream itself.
    46	        Pedantic bool `json:"pedantic,omitempty"`
    47	}
    48	
    49	// StreamConfig will determine the name, subjects and retention policy
    50	// for a given stream. If subjects is empty the name will be used.
    51	type StreamConfig struct {
    52	        Name         string           `json:"name"`
    53	        Description  string           `json:"description,omitempty"`
    54	        Subjects     []string         `json:"subjects,omitempty"`
    55	        Retention    RetentionPolicy  `json:"retention"`
    56	        MaxConsumers int              `json:"max_consumers"`
    57	        MaxMsgs      int64            `json:"max_msgs"`
    58	        MaxBytes     int64            `json:"max_bytes"`
    59	        MaxAge       time.Duration    `json:"max_age"`
    60	        MaxMsgsPer   int64            `json:"max_msgs_per_subject"`
    61	        MaxMsgSize   int32            `json:"max_msg_size,omitempty"`
    62	        Discard      DiscardPolicy    `json:"discard"`
    63	        Storage      StorageType      `json:"storage"`
    64	        Replicas     int              `json:"num_replicas"`
    65	        NoAck        bool             `json:"no_ack,omitempty"`
    66	        Template     string           `json:"template_owner,omitempty"` // Deprecated: stream templates are deprecated and will be removed in a future version.
    67	        Duplicates   time.Duration    `json:"duplicate_window,omitempty"`
    68	        Placement    *Placement       `json:"placement,omitempty"`
    69	        Mirror       *StreamSource    `json:"mirror,omitempty"`
    70	        Sources      []*StreamSource  `json:"sources,omitempty"`
    71	        Compression  StoreCompression `json:"compression"`
    72	        FirstSeq     uint64           `json:"first_seq,omitempty"`
    73	
    74	        // Allow applying a subject transform to incoming messages before doing anything else
    75	        SubjectTransform *SubjectTransformConfig `json:"subject_transform,omitempty"`
    76	
    77	        // Allow republish of the message after being sequenced and stored.
    78	        RePublish *RePublish `json:"republish,omitempty"`
    79	
    80	        // Allow higher performance, direct access to get individual messages. E.g. KeyValue
    81	        AllowDirect bool `json:"allow_direct"`
    82	        // Allow higher performance and unified direct access for mirrors as well.
    83	        MirrorDirect bool `json:"mirror_direct"`
    84	
    85	        // Allow KV like semantics to also discard new on a per subject basis
    86	        DiscardNewPer bool `json:"discard_new_per_subject,omitempty"`
    87	
    88	        // Optional qualifiers. These can not be modified after set to true.
    89	
    90	        // Sealed will seal a stream so no messages can get out or in.
    91	        Sealed bool `json:"sealed"`
    92	        // DenyDelete will restrict the ability to delete messages.
    93	        DenyDelete bool `json:"deny_delete"`
    94	        // DenyPurge will restrict the ability to purge messages.
    95	        DenyPurge bool `json:"deny_purge"`
    96	        // AllowRollup allows messages to be placed into the system and purge

   126	// clone performs a deep copy of the StreamConfig struct, returning a new clone with
   127	// all values copied.
   128	func (cfg *StreamConfig) clone() *StreamConfig {
   129	        clone := *cfg
   130	        if cfg.Placement != nil {
   131	                placement := *cfg.Placement
   132	                clone.Placement = &placement
   133	        }
   134	        if cfg.Mirror != nil {
   135	                mirror := *cfg.Mirror
   136	                clone.Mirror = &mirror
   137	        }

   139	                clone.Sources = make([]*StreamSource, len(cfg.Sources))
   140	                for i, cfgSource := range cfg.Sources {
   141	                        source := *cfgSource
   142	                        clone.Sources[i] = &source
   143	                }

  7550	// This will be returned to external callers.
  7551	func (fs *fileStore) msgForSeq(seq uint64, sm *StoreMsg) (*StoreMsg, error) {
  7552	        return fs.msgForSeqLocked(seq, sm, true)
  7553	}
  7554	
  7555	// Will return message for the given sequence number.
  7556	func (fs *fileStore) msgForSeqLocked(seq uint64, sm *StoreMsg, needFSLock bool) (*StoreMsg, error) {
  7557	        // TODO(dlc) - Since Store, Remove, Skip all hold the write lock on fs this will
  7558	        // be stalled. Need another lock if want to happen in parallel.
  7559	        if needFSLock {
  7560	                fs.mu.RLock()
  7561	        }
  7562	        if fs.closed {
  7563	                if needFSLock {
  7564	                        fs.mu.RUnlock()
  7565	                }
  7566	                return nil, ErrStoreClosed
  7567	        }
  7568	        // Indicates we want first msg.
  7569	        if seq == 0 {
  7570	                seq = fs.state.FirstSeq
  7571	        }
  7572	        // Make sure to snapshot here.
  7573	        mb, lseq := fs.selectMsgBlock(seq), fs.state.LastSeq
  7574	        if needFSLock {
  7575	                fs.mu.RUnlock()
  7576	        }
  7577	
  7578	        if mb == nil {
  7579	                var err = ErrStoreEOF
  7580	                if seq <= lseq {
  7581	                        err = ErrStoreMsgNotFound
  7582	                }
  7583	                return nil, err
  7584	        }
  7585	
  7586	        fsm, expireOk, err := mb.fetchMsg(seq, sm)
  7587	        if err != nil {
  7588	                return nil, err
  7589	        }
  7590	
  7591	        // We detected a linear scan and access to the last message.
  7592	        // If we are not the last message block we can try to expire the cache.
  7593	        if expireOk {
  7594	                mb.tryForceExpireCache()
  7595	        }
  7596	
  7597	        return fsm, nil
  7598	}
  7599

  1591	func (ms *memStore) LoadMsg(seq uint64, smp *StoreMsg) (*StoreMsg, error) {
  1592	        return ms.loadMsgLocked(seq, smp, true)
  1593	}
  1594	
  1595	// loadMsgLocked will lookup the message by sequence number and return it if found.
  1596	func (ms *memStore) loadMsgLocked(seq uint64, smp *StoreMsg, needMSLock bool) (*StoreMsg, error) {
  1597	        if needMSLock {
  1598	                ms.mu.RLock()
  1599	        }
  1600	        sm, ok := ms.msgs[seq]
  1601	        last := ms.state.LastSeq
  1602	        if needMSLock {
  1603	                ms.mu.RUnlock()
  1604	        }
  1605	
  1606	        if !ok || sm == nil {
  1607	                var err = ErrStoreEOF
  1608	                if seq <= last {
  1609	                        err = ErrStoreMsgNotFound
  1610	                }
  1611	                return nil, err
  1612	        }
  1613	
  1614	        if smp == nil {
  1615	                smp = new(StoreMsg)
  1616	        }
  1617	        sm.copy(smp)
  1618	        return smp, nil
  1619	}

   188	func (r *JSPubAckResponse) ToError() error {

   573	func (a *Account) addStreamWithAssignment(config *StreamConfig, fsConfig *FileStoreConfig, sa *streamAssignment, pedantic bool) (*stream, error) {
   574	        s, jsa, err := a.checkForJetStream()
   575	        if err != nil {
   576	                return nil, err
   577	        }
   578	
   579	        // If we do not have the stream currently assigned to us in cluster mode we will proceed but warn.
   580	        // This can happen on startup with restored state where on meta replay we still do not have
   581	        // the assignment. Running in single server mode this always returns true.

   583	                s.Debugf("Stream '%s > %s' does not seem to be assigned to this server", a.Name, config.Name)
   584	        }

   586	        // Sensible defaults.
   587	        ccfg, apiErr := s.checkStreamCfg(config, a, pedantic)
   588	        if apiErr != nil {
   589	                return nil, apiErr
   590	        }
   591	        cfg := &ccfg
   592	
   593	        singleServerMode := !s.JetStreamIsClustered() && s.standAloneMode()
   594	        if singleServerMode && cfg.Replicas > 1 {
   595	                return nil, ApiErrors[JSStreamReplicasNotSupportedErr]
   596	        }

   880	
   881	        // Call directly to set leader if not in clustered mode.
   882	        // This can be called though before we actually setup clustering, so check both.
   883	        if singleServerMode {
   884	                if err := mset.setLeader(true); err != nil {
   885	                        mset.stop(true, false)
   886	                        return nil, err
   887	                }
   888	        }
   889	
   890	        // This is always true in single server mode.
   891	        if mset.IsLeader() {
   892	                // Send advisory.
   893	                var suppress bool
   894	                if !s.standAloneMode() && sa == nil {
   895	                        if cfg.Replicas > 1 {
   896	                                suppress = true
   897	                        }
   898	                } else if sa != nil {
   899	                        suppress = sa.responded
   900	                }
   901	                if !suppress {
   902	                        mset.sendCreateAdvisory()
   903	                }
   904	        }
   905	
   906	        // Register with our account last.
   907	        jsa.mu.Lock()
   908	        jsa.streams[cfg.Name] = mset
   909	        jsa.mu.Unlock()
   910

     1	// Copyright 2025 The NATS Authors

    11	// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and

    14	package server
    15	
    16	import (
    17	        "encoding/json"
    18	        "errors"
    19	        "fmt"
    20	        "math"
    21	        "math/big"
    22	        "path/filepath"
    23	        "slices"
    24	        "strings"
    25	        "sync"
    26	        "sync/atomic"
    27	        "time"
    28	)
    29	
    30	var (
    31	        // Tracks the total inflight batches, across all streams and accounts that enable batching.
    32	        globalInflightBatches atomic.Int32
    33	)
    34	
    35	type batching struct {
    36	        mu    sync.Mutex
    37	        group map[string]*batchGroup
    38	}
    39	
    40	type batchGroup struct {
    41	        lseq  uint64
    42	        store StreamStore
    43	        timer *time.Timer
    44	}
    45	
    46	// Lock should be held.
    47	func (batches *batching) newBatchGroup(mset *stream, batchId string) (*batchGroup, error) {
    48	        store, err := newBatchStore(mset, batchId)
    49	        if err != nil {
    50	                return nil, err
    51	        }
    52	        b := &batchGroup{store: store}
    53	
    54	        // Create a timer to clean up after timeout.
    55	        timeout := streamMaxBatchTimeout
    56	        if maxBatchTimeout := mset.srv.getOpts().JetStreamLimits.MaxBatchTimeout; maxBatchTimeout > 0 {
    57	                timeout = maxBatchTimeout
    58	        }
    59	        b.timer = time.AfterFunc(timeout, func() {
    60	                b.cleanup(batchId, batches)
    61	        })
    62	        return b, nil
    63	}
    64	
    65	func getBatchStoreDir(mset *stream, batchId string) (string, string) {
    66	        mset.mu.RLock()
    67	        jsa, name := mset.jsa, mset.cfg.Name
    68	        mset.mu.RUnlock()
    69	
    70	        jsa.mu.RLock()
    71	        sd := jsa.storeDir
    72	        jsa.mu.RUnlock()
    73	
    74	        bname := getHash(batchId)
    75	        return bname, filepath.Join(sd, streamsDir, name, batchesDir, bname)
    76	}
    77	
    78	func newBatchStore(mset *stream, batchId string) (StreamStore, error) {
    79	        mset.mu.RLock()
    80	        replicas, storage := mset.cfg.Replicas, mset.cfg.Storage
    81	        mset.mu.RUnlock()
    82	
    83	        if replicas == 1 && storage == FileStorage {
    84	                bname, storeDir := getBatchStoreDir(mset, batchId)
    85	                fcfg := FileStoreConfig{AsyncFlush: true, BlockSize: defaultLargeBlockSize, StoreDir: storeDir}
    86	                s := mset.srv
    87	                prf := s.jsKeyGen(s.getOpts().JetStreamKey, mset.acc.Name)
    88	                if prf != nil {
    89	                        // We are encrypted here, fill in correct cipher selection.
    90	                        fcfg.Cipher = s.getOpts().JetStreamCipher
    91	                }
    92	                oldprf := s.jsKeyGen(s.getOpts().JetStreamOldKey, mset.acc.Name)
    93	                cfg := StreamConfig{Name: bname, Storage: FileStorage}
    94	                return newFileStoreWithCreated(fcfg, cfg, time.Time{}, prf, oldprf)
    95	        }
    96	        return newMemStore(&StreamConfig{Name: _EMPTY_, Storage: MemoryStorage})
    97	}
    98	
    99	// readyForCommit indicates the batch is ready to be committed.
   100	// If the timer has already cleaned up the batch, we can't commit.
   101	// Otherwise, we ensure the timer does not clean up the batch in the meantime.
   102	// Lock should be held.
   103	func (b *batchGroup) readyForCommit() bool {
   104	        if !b.timer.Stop() {
   105	                return false
   106	        }
   107	        b.store.FlushAllPending()
   108	        return true
   109	}
   110	
   111	// cleanup deletes underlying resources associated with the batch and unregisters it from the stream's batches.
   112	func (b *batchGroup) cleanup(batchId string, batches *batching) {
   113	        batches.mu.Lock()
   114	        defer batches.mu.Unlock()
   115	        b.cleanupLocked(batchId, batches)
   116	}
   117	
   118	// Lock should be held.
   119	func (b *batchGroup) cleanupLocked(batchId string, batches *batching) {
   120	        globalInflightBatches.Add(-1)
   121	        b.timer.Stop()
   122	        b.store.Delete(true)
   123	        delete(batches.group, batchId)
   124	}
   125	
   126	// Lock should be held.
   127	func (b *batchGroup) stopLocked() {
   128	        globalInflightBatches.Add(-1)
   129	        b.timer.Stop()
   130	        b.store.Stop()
   131	}
   132	
   133	// batchStagedDiff stages all changes for consistency checks until commit.
   134	type batchStagedDiff struct {
   135	        msgIds             map[string]struct{}
   136	        counter            map[string]*msgCounterRunningTotal
   137	        inflight           map[string]*inflightSubjectRunningTotal
   138	        expectedPerSubject map[string]*batchExpectedPerSubject
   139	}
   140	
   141	type batchExpectedPerSubject struct {
   142	        sseq  uint64 // Stream sequence.
   143	        clseq uint64 // Clustered proposal sequence.
   144	}
   145	
   146	func (diff *batchStagedDiff) commit(mset *stream) {
   147	        if len(diff.msgIds) > 0 {
   148	                ts := time.Now().UnixNano()
   149	                mset.ddMu.Lock()
   150	                for msgId := range diff.msgIds {
   151	                        // We stage with zero, and will update in processJetStreamMsg once we know the sequence.
   152	                        mset.storeMsgIdLocked(&ddentry{msgId, 0, ts})
   153	                }
   154	                mset.ddMu.Unlock()
   155	        }
   156	
   157	        // Store running totals for counters, we could have multiple counter increments proposed, but not applied yet.
   158	        if len(diff.counter) > 0 {
   159	                if mset.clusteredCounterTotal == nil {
   160	                        mset.clusteredCounterTotal = make(map[string]*msgCounterRunningTotal, len(diff.counter))
   161	                }
   162	                for k, c := range diff.counter {
   163	                        mset.clusteredCounterTotal[k] = c
   164	                }
   165	        }
   166	
   167	        // Track inflight.
   168	        if len(diff.inflight) > 0 {
   169	                if mset.inflight == nil {
   170	                        mset.inflight = make(map[string]*inflightSubjectRunningTotal, len(diff.inflight))
   171	                }
   172	                for subj, i := range diff.inflight {
   173	                        if c, ok := mset.inflight[subj]; ok {
   174	                                c.bytes += i.bytes
   175	                                c.ops += i.ops
   176	                        } else {
   177	                                mset.inflight[subj] = i
   178	                        }
   179	                }
   180	        }
   181	
   182	        // Track sequence and subject.
   183	        if len(diff.expectedPerSubject) > 0 {
   184	                if mset.expectedPerSubjectSequence == nil {
   185	                        mset.expectedPerSubjectSequence = make(map[uint64]string, len(diff.expectedPerSubject))
   186	                }
   187	                if mset.expectedPerSubjectInProcess == nil {
   188	                        mset.expectedPerSubjectInProcess = make(map[string]struct{}, len(diff.expectedPerSubject))
   189	                }
   190	                for subj, e := range diff.expectedPerSubject {
   191	                        mset.expectedPerSubjectSequence[e.clseq] = subj
   192	                        mset.expectedPerSubjectInProcess[subj] = struct{}{}
   193	                }
   194	        }
   195	}
   196	
   197	type batchApply struct {
   198	        mu         sync.Mutex
   199	        id         string            // ID of the current batch.
   200	        count      uint64            // Number of entries in the batch, for consistency checks.
   201	        entries    []*CommittedEntry // Previous entries that are part of this batch.
   202	        entryStart int               // The index into an entry indicating the first message of the batch.
   203	        maxApplied uint64            // Applied value before the entry containing the first message of the batch.
   204	}
   205	
   206	// clearBatchStateLocked clears in-memory apply-batch-related state.
   207	// batch.mu lock should be held.
   208	func (batch *batchApply) clearBatchStateLocked() {
   209	        batch.id = _EMPTY_
   210	        batch.count = 0
   211	        batch.entries = nil
   212	        batch.entryStart = 0
   213	        batch.maxApplied = 0
   214	}
   215	
   216	// rejectBatchStateLocked rejects the batch and clears in-memory apply-batch-related state.
   217	// Corrects mset.clfs to take the failed batch into account.
   218	// batch.mu lock should be held.
   219	func (batch *batchApply) rejectBatchStateLocked(mset *stream) {
   220	        mset.clMu.Lock()
   221	        mset.clfs += batch.count
   222	        mset.clMu.Unlock()
   223	        // We're rejecting the batch, so all entries need to be returned to the pool.
   224	        for _, bce := range batch.entries {
   225	                bce.ReturnToPool()
   226	        }
   227	        batch.clearBatchStateLocked()
   228	}
   229	
   230	func (batch *batchApply) rejectBatchState(mset *stream) {
   231	        batch.mu.Lock()
   232	        defer batch.mu.Unlock()
   233	        batch.rejectBatchStateLocked(mset)
   234	}
   235	
   236	// checkMsgHeadersPreClusteredProposal checks the message for expected/consistency headers.
   237	// mset.mu lock must NOT be held or used.
   238	// mset.clMu lock must be held.
   239	func checkMsgHeadersPreClusteredProposal(
   240	        diff *batchStagedDiff, mset *stream, subject string, hdr []byte, msg []byte, sourced bool, name string,
   241	        jsa *jsAccount, allowRollup, denyPurge, allowTTL, allowMsgCounter, allowMsgSchedules bool,
   242	        discard DiscardPolicy, discardNewPer bool, maxMsgSize int, maxMsgs int64, maxMsgsPer int64, maxBytes int64,
   243	) ([]byte, []byte, uint64, *ApiError, error) {
   244	        var incr *big.Int
   245	
   246	        // Some header checks must be checked pre proposal.
   247	        if len(hdr) > 0 {
   248	                // Since we encode header len as u16 make sure we do not exceed.
   249	                // Again this works if it goes through but better to be pre-emptive.
   250	                if len(hdr) > math.MaxUint16 {
   251	                        err := fmt.Errorf("JetStream header size exceeds limits for '%s > %s'", jsa.acc().Name, mset.cfg.Name)
   252	                        return hdr, msg, 0, NewJSStreamHeaderExceedsMaximumError(), err
   253	                }
   254	                // Counter increments.
   255	                // Only supported on counter streams, and payload must be empty (if not coming from a source).
   256	                var ok bool
   257	                if incr, ok = getMessageIncr(hdr); !ok {
   258	                        apiErr := NewJSMessageIncrInvalidError()
   259	                        return hdr, msg, 0, apiErr, apiErr
   260	                } else if incr != nil && !sourced {
   261	                        // Only do checks if the message isn't sourced. Otherwise, we need to store verbatim.
   262	                        if !allowMsgCounter {
   263	                                apiErr := NewJSMessageIncrDisabledError()
   264	                                return hdr, msg, 0, apiErr, apiErr
   265	                        } else if len(msg) > 0 {
   266	                                apiErr := NewJSMessageIncrPayloadError()
   267	                                return hdr, msg, 0, apiErr, apiErr
   268	                        } else {
   269	                                // Check for incompatible headers.
   270	                                var doErr bool
   271	                                if getRollup(hdr) != _EMPTY_ ||
   272	                                        getExpectedStream(hdr) != _EMPTY_ ||
   273	                                        getExpectedLastMsgId(hdr) != _EMPTY_ ||
   274	                                        getExpectedLastSeqPerSubjectForSubject(hdr) != _EMPTY_ {
   275	                                        doErr = true
   276	                                } else if _, ok = getExpectedLastSeq(hdr); ok {
   277	                                        doErr = true
   278	                                } else if _, ok = getExpectedLastSeqPerSubject(hdr); ok {
   279	                                        doErr = true
   280	                                }
   281	
   282	                                if doErr {
   283	                                        apiErr := NewJSMessageIncrInvalidError()
   284	                                        return hdr, msg, 0, apiErr, apiErr
   285	                                }
   286	                        }
   287	                }
   288	                // Expected stream name can also be pre-checked.
   289	                if sname := getExpectedStream(hdr); sname != _EMPTY_ && sname != name {
   290	                        return hdr, msg, 0, NewJSStreamNotMatchError(), errStreamMismatch
   291	                }
   292	                // TTL'd messages are rejected entirely if TTLs are not enabled on the stream, or if the TTL is invalid.
   293	                if ttl, err := getMessageTTL(hdr); !sourced && (ttl != 0 || err != nil) {
   294	                        if !allowTTL {
   295	                                return hdr, msg, 0, NewJSMessageTTLDisabledError(), errMsgTTLDisabled
   296	                        } else if err != nil {
   297	                                return hdr, msg, 0, NewJSMessageTTLInvalidError(), err
   298	                        }
   299	                }
   300	                // Check for MsgIds here at the cluster level to avoid excessive CLFS accounting.
   301	                // Will help during restarts.
   302	                if msgId := getMsgId(hdr); msgId != _EMPTY_ {
   303	                        // Dedupe if staged.
   304	                        if _, ok = diff.msgIds[msgId]; ok {
   305	                                return hdr, msg, 0, nil, errMsgIdDuplicate
   306	                        }
   307	                        mset.ddMu.Lock()
   308	                        if dde := mset.checkMsgId(msgId); dde != nil {
   309	                                seq := dde.seq
   310	                                mset.ddMu.Unlock()
   311	                                // Should not return an invalid sequence, in that case error.
   312	                                if seq > 0 {
   313	                                        return hdr, msg, seq, nil, errMsgIdDuplicate
   314	                                } else {
   315	                                        return hdr, msg, 0, NewJSStreamDuplicateMessageConflictError(), errMsgIdDuplicate
   316	                                }
   317	                        }
   318	                        if diff.msgIds == nil {
   319	                                diff.msgIds = map[string]struct{}{msgId: {}}
   320	                        } else {
   321	                                diff.msgIds[msgId] = struct{}{}
   322	                        }
   323	                        mset.ddMu.Unlock()
   324	                }
   325	        }
   326	
   327	        // Apply increment for counter.
   328	        // But only if it's allowed for this stream. This can happen when we store verbatim for a sourced stream.
   329	        if incr == nil && allowMsgCounter {
   330	                apiErr := NewJSMessageIncrMissingError()
   331	                return hdr, msg, 0, apiErr, apiErr
   332	        }
   333	        if incr != nil && allowMsgCounter {
   334	                var initial big.Int
   335	                var sources CounterSources
   336	
   337	                // If we've got a running total, update that, since we have inflight proposals updating the same counter.
   338	                var ok bool
   339	                var counter *msgCounterRunningTotal
   340	                if counter, ok = diff.counter[subject]; ok {
   341	                        initial = *counter.total
   342	                        sources = counter.sources
   343	                } else if counter, ok = mset.clusteredCounterTotal[subject]; ok {
   344	                        initial = *counter.total
   345	                        sources = counter.sources
   346	                        // Make an explicit copy to separate the staged data from what's committed.
   347	                        // Don't need to initialize all values, they'll be overwritten later.
   348	                        counter = &msgCounterRunningTotal{ops: counter.ops}
   349	                } else {
   350	                        // Load last message, and store as inflight running total.
   351	                        var smv StoreMsg
   352	                        sm, err := mset.store.LoadLastMsg(subject, &smv)
   353	                        if err == nil && sm != nil {
   354	                                var val CounterValue
   355	                                // Return an error if the counter is broken somehow.
   356	                                if json.Unmarshal(sm.msg, &val) != nil {
   357	                                        apiErr := NewJSMessageCounterBrokenError()
   358	                                        return hdr, msg, 0, apiErr, apiErr
   359	                                }
   360	                                if ncs := sliceHeader(JSMessageCounterSources, sm.hdr); len(ncs) > 0 {
   361	                                        if err := json.Unmarshal(ncs, &sources); err != nil {
   362	                                                apiErr := NewJSMessageCounterBrokenError()
   363	                                                return hdr, msg, 0, apiErr, apiErr
   364	                                        }
   365	                                }
   366	                                initial.SetString(val.Value, 10)
   367	                        }
   368	                }
   369	                srchdr := sliceHeader(JSStreamSource, hdr)
   370	                if len(srchdr) > 0 {
   371	                        // This is a sourced message, so we can't apply Nats-Incr but
   372	                        // instead should just update the source count header.
   373	                        fields := strings.Split(string(srchdr), " ")
   374	                        origStream := fields[0]
   375	                        origSubj := subject
   376	                        if len(fields) >= 5 {
   377	                                origSubj = fields[4]
   378	                        }
   379	                        var val CounterValue
   380	                        if json.Unmarshal(msg, &val) != nil {
   381	                                apiErr := NewJSMessageCounterBrokenError()
   382	                                return hdr, msg, 0, apiErr, apiErr
   383	                        }
   384	                        var sourced big.Int
   385	                        sourced.SetString(val.Value, 10)
   386	                        if sources == nil {
   387	                                sources = map[string]map[string]string{}
   388	                        }
   389	                        if _, ok = sources[origStream]; !ok {
   390	                                sources[origStream] = map[string]string{}
   391	                        }
   392	                        prevVal := sources[origStream][origSubj]
   393	                        sources[origStream][origSubj] = sourced.String()
   394	                        // We will also replace the Nats-Incr header with the diff
   395	                        // between our last value from this source and this one, so
   396	                        // that the arithmetic is always correct.
   397	                        var previous big.Int
   398	                        previous.SetString(prevVal, 10)
   399	                        incr.Sub(&sourced, &previous)
   400	                        hdr = setHeader(JSMessageIncr, incr.String(), hdr)
   401	                }
   402	                // Now make the change.
   403	                initial.Add(&initial, incr)
   404	                // Generate the new payload.
   405	                var _msg [128]byte
   406	                msg = fmt.Appendf(_msg[:0], "{%q:%q}", "val", initial.String())
   407	                // Write the updated source count headers.
   408	                if len(sources) > 0 {
   409	                        nhdr, err := json.Marshal(sources)
   410	                        if err != nil {
   411	                                return hdr, msg, 0, NewJSMessageCounterBrokenError(), err
   412	                        }
   413	                        hdr = setHeader(JSMessageCounterSources, string(nhdr), hdr)
   414	                }
   415	
   416	                // Check to see if we are over the max msg size.
   417	                maxSize := int64(mset.srv.getOpts().MaxPayload)
   418	                if maxMsgSize >= 0 && int64(maxMsgSize) < maxSize {
   419	                        maxSize = int64(maxMsgSize)
   420	                }
   421	                hdrLen, msgLen := int64(len(hdr)), int64(len(msg))
   422	                // Subtract to prevent against overflows.
   423	                if hdrLen > maxSize || msgLen > maxSize-hdrLen {
   424	                        return hdr, msg, 0, NewJSStreamMessageExceedsMaximumError(), ErrMaxPayload
   425	                }
   426	
   427	                // Keep the in-memory counters up-to-date.
   428	                if counter == nil {
   429	                        counter = &msgCounterRunningTotal{}
   430	                }
   431	                counter.total = &initial
   432	                counter.sources = sources
   433	                counter.ops++
   434	                if diff.counter == nil {
   435	                        diff.counter = map[string]*msgCounterRunningTotal{subject: counter}
   436	                } else {
   437	                        diff.counter[subject] = counter
   438	                }
   439	        }
   440	
   441	        if len(hdr) > 0 {
   442	                // Expected last sequence.
   443	                if seq, exists := getExpectedLastSeq(hdr); exists && seq != mset.clseq-mset.clfs {
   444	                        mlseq := mset.clseq - mset.clfs
   445	                        err := fmt.Errorf("last sequence mismatch: %d vs %d", seq, mlseq)
   446	                        return hdr, msg, 0, NewJSStreamWrongLastSequenceError(mlseq), err
   447	                }
   448	
   449	                // Expected last sequence per subject.
   450	                if seq, exists := getExpectedLastSeqPerSubject(hdr); exists {
   451	                        // Allow override of the subject used for the check.
   452	                        seqSubj := subject
   453	                        if optSubj := getExpectedLastSeqPerSubjectForSubject(hdr); optSubj != _EMPTY_ {
   454	                                seqSubj = optSubj
   455	                        }
   456	
   457	                        // The subject is already written to in this batch, we can't allow
   458	                        // expected checks since they would be incorrect.
   459	                        if _, ok := diff.inflight[seqSubj]; ok {
   460	                                err := errors.New("last sequence by subject mismatch")
   461	                                return hdr, msg, 0, NewJSStreamWrongLastSequenceConstantError(), err
   462	                        }
   463	
   464	                        // If the subject is already in process, block as otherwise we could have
   465	                        // multiple messages inflight with the same subject.
   466	                        if _, found := mset.expectedPerSubjectInProcess[seqSubj]; found {
   467	                                err := errors.New("last sequence by subject mismatch")
   468	                                return hdr, msg, 0, NewJSStreamWrongLastSequenceConstantError(), err
   469	                        }
   470	
   471	                        // If the subject is already in process but without expected headers, block as we would have
   472	                        // multiple messages inflight with the same subject.
   473	                        if _, ok := mset.inflight[seqSubj]; ok {
   474	                                err := errors.New("last sequence by subject mismatch")<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you.   4337	// Lock does NOT need to be held, we set the client on setup and never change it at this point.
  4338	func (mset *stream) subscribeInternal(subject string, cb msgHandler) (*subscription, error) {
  4339	        if mset.closed.Load() {
  4340	                return nil, errStreamClosed
  4341	        }
  4342	        if cb == nil {
  4343	                return nil, errInvalidMsgHandler
  4344	        }
  4345	        c := mset.client
  4346	        sid := int(mset.sid.Add(1))
  4347	        // Now create the subscription
  4348	        return c.processSub([]byte(subject), nil, []byte(strconv.Itoa(sid)), cb, false)
  4349	}

  4352	func (mset *stream) queueSubscribeInternal(subject, group string, cb msgHandler) (*subscription, error) {

  4354	                return nil, errStreamClosed
  4355	        }
  4356	        if cb == nil {
  4357	                return nil, errInvalidMsgHandler
  4358	        }
  4359	        c := mset.client
  4360	        sid := int(mset.sid.Add(1))
  4361	        // Now create the subscription
  4362	        return c.processSub([]byte(subject), []byte(group), []byte(strconv.Itoa(sid)), cb, false)

  4365	// This will unsubscribe us from the exact subject given.
  4366	// We do not currently track the subs so do not have the sid.
  4367	// This should be called only on an update.
  4368	// Lock does NOT need to be held, we set the client on setup and never change it at this point.
  4369	func (mset *stream) unsubscribeInternal(subject string) error {
  4370	        if mset.closed.Load() {
  4371	                return errStreamClosed
  4372	        }
  4373	        c := mset.client
  4374	        var sid []byte
  4375	        c.mu.Lock()
  4376	        for _, sub := range c.subs {
  4377	                if subject == string(sub.subject) {
  4378	                        sid = sub.sid
  4379	                        break
  4380	                }
  4381	        }
  4382	        c.mu.Unlock()
  4383	
  4384	        if sid != nil {
  4385	                return c.processUnsub(sid)
  4386	        }
  4387	        return nil
  4388	}
  4389	
  4390	// Lock should be held.
  4391	func (mset *stream) unsubscribe(sub *subscription) {
  4392	        if sub == nil || mset.closed.Load() {
  4393	                return
  4394	        }
  4395	        mset.client.processUnsub(sub.sid)
  4396	}

  1050	func (mset *stream) isLeaderNodeState() bool {
  1051	        if mset.isClustered() {
  1052	                return mset.node.State() == Leader
  1053	        }
  1054	        return true
  1055	}
  1056	
  1057	// TODO(dlc) - Check to see if we can accept being the leader or we should step down.
  1058	func (mset *stream) setLeader(isLeader bool) error {
  1059	        mset.mu.Lock()
  1060	        // If we are here we have a change in leader status.
  1061	        if isLeader {
  1062	                // Make sure we are listening for sync requests.
  1063	                // TODO(dlc) - Original design was that all in sync members of the group would do DQ.
  1064	                if mset.isClustered() {
  1065	                        mset.startClusterSubs()
  1066	                }
  1067	
  1068	                // Setup subscriptions if we were not already the leader.
  1069	                if err := mset.subscribeToStream(); err != nil {
  1070	                        if mset.isClustered() {
  1071	                                // Stepdown since we have an error.
  1072	                                mset.node.StepDown()
  1073	                        }
  1074	                        mset.mu.Unlock()
  1075	                        return err
  1076	                }
  1077	        } else {
  1078	                // cancel timer to create the source consumers if not fired yet
  1079	                if mset.sourcesConsumerSetup != nil {
  1080	                        mset.sourcesConsumerSetup.Stop()
  1081	                        mset.sourcesConsumerSetup = nil
  1082	                } else {
  1083	                        // Stop any source consumers
  1084	                        mset.stopSourceConsumers()
  1085	                }
  1086	
  1087	                // Stop responding to sync requests.
  1088	                mset.stopClusterSubs()
  1089	                // Unsubscribe from direct stream.
  1090	                mset.unsubscribeToStream(false, false)

  4113	        if mset.active {
  4114	                return nil
  4115	        }
  4116	        for _, subject := range mset.cfg.Subjects {
  4117	                if _, err := mset.subscribeInternal(subject, mset.processInboundJetStreamMsg); err != nil {
  4118	                        return err
  4119	                }
  4120	        }
  4121	        // Check if we need to setup mirroring.
  4122	        if mset.cfg.Mirror != nil {
  4123	                // setup the initial mirror sourceInfo
  4124	                mset.mirror = &sourceInfo{name: mset.cfg.Mirror.Name}
  4125	                sfs := make([]string, len(mset.cfg.Mirror.SubjectTransforms))
  4126	                trs := make([]*subjectTransform, len(mset.cfg.Mirror.SubjectTransforms))
  4127

  4133	                        }
  4134

  4136	                        trs[i] = subjectTransform
  4137	                }
  4138	                mset.mirror.sfs = sfs
  4139	                mset.mirror.trs = trs
  4140	                // delay the actual mirror consumer creation for after a delay
  4141	                mset.scheduleSetupMirrorConsumerRetry()
  4142	        } else if len(mset.cfg.Sources) > 0 && mset.sourcesConsumerSetup == nil {
  4143	                // Setup the initial source infos for the sources
  4144	                mset.resetSourceInfo()

  4153	                                mset.mu.Unlock()
  4154	                        })

  4167	                        }

  4171	                }
  4172	        }

  4175	        return nil

  4793	func (mset *stream) processDirectGetRequest(_ *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subject, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
  4794	        if len(reply) == 0 {
  4795	                return
  4796	        }
  4797	        hdr, msg := c.msgParts(rmsg)
  4798	        if errorOnRequiredApiLevel(hdr) {
  4799	                hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 412 Required Api Level\r\n\r\n")
  4800	                mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4801	                return
  4802	        }
  4803	        if len(msg) == 0 {
  4804	                hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 408 Empty Request\r\n\r\n")
  4805	                mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4806	                return
  4807	        }
  4808	        var req JSApiMsgGetRequest
  4809	        err := json.Unmarshal(msg, &req)
  4810	        if err != nil {
  4811	                hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 408 Malformed Request\r\n\r\n")
  4812	                mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4813	                return
  4814	        }
  4815	        // Check if nothing set.
  4816	        if req.Seq == 0 && req.LastFor == _EMPTY_ && req.NextFor == _EMPTY_ && len(req.MultiLastFor) == 0 && req.StartTime == nil {
  4817	                hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 408 Empty Request\r\n\r\n")
  4818	                mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4819	                return
  4820	        }
  4821	        // Check we don't have conflicting options set.
  4822	        // We do not allow batch mode for lastFor requests.
  4823	        if (req.Seq > 0 && req.LastFor != _EMPTY_) ||
  4824	                (req.Seq > 0 && req.StartTime != nil) ||
  4825	                (req.StartTime != nil && req.LastFor != _EMPTY_) ||
  4826	                (req.LastFor != _EMPTY_ && req.NextFor != _EMPTY_) ||
  4827	                (req.LastFor != _EMPTY_ && req.Batch > 0) ||
  4828	                (req.LastFor != _EMPTY_ && len(req.MultiLastFor) > 0) ||
  4829	                (req.NextFor != _EMPTY_ && len(req.MultiLastFor) > 0) ||
  4830	                (req.UpToSeq > 0 && req.UpToTime != nil) {
  4831	                hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 408 Bad Request\r\n\r\n")
  4832	                mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4833	                return
  4834	        }
  4835	
  4836	        // Reject request if we can't guarantee the precondition of min last sequence.
  4837	        if req.MinLastSeq > 0 && mset.lastSeq() < req.MinLastSeq {
  4838	                // We are not up-to-date yet, and don't know how long it will take us to be.
  4839	
  4840	                // If we're not the leader, we can redirect to the leader for them to answer.
  4841	                // But only on the original stream, mirrors only error.
  4842	                if !mset.isMirror() && !mset.isLeaderNodeState() {
  4843	                        // Copy since we're sending the same bytes.
  4844	                        msg = copyBytes(msg)
  4845	                        dsubj := fmt.Sprintf(JSDirectLeaderMsgGetT, mset.getCfgName())
  4846	                        mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(dsubj, _EMPTY_, reply, nil, msg, nil, 0))
  4847	                        return
  4848	                }
  4849	
  4850	                // Otherwise, simply reject the request so the client can retry.

  5067	func (mset *stream) getDirectRequest(req *JSApiMsgGetRequest, reply string) {
  5068	        // Handle multi in separate function.
  5069	        if len(req.MultiLastFor) > 0 {
  5070	                mset.getDirectMulti(req, reply)
  5071	                return
  5072	        }
  5073	
  5074	        mset.mu.RLock()
  5075	        store, name, s := mset.store, mset.cfg.Name, mset.srv
  5076	        mset.mu.RUnlock()
  5077	
  5078	        var seq uint64
  5079	        // Lookup start seq if AsOfTime is set.
  5080	        if req.StartTime != nil {
  5081	                seq = store.GetSeqFromTime(*req.StartTime)
  5082	        } else {
  5083	                seq = req.Seq
  5084	        }
  5085	
  5086	        wc := subjectHasWildcard(req.NextFor)
  5087	        // For tracking num pending if we are batch.
  5088	        var np, lseq, validThrough uint64
  5089	        var isBatchRequest bool
  5090	        batch := req.Batch
  5091	        if batch == 0 {
  5092	                batch = 1
  5093	        } else {
  5094	                // This is a batch request, capture initial numPending.
  5095	                isBatchRequest = true
  5096	                np, validThrough = store.NumPending(seq, req.NextFor, false)
  5097	        }
  5098	
  5099	        // Grab MaxBytes
  5100	        mb := req.MaxBytes
  5101	        if mb == 0 && s != nil {
  5102	                // Fill in with the server's MaxPending.
  5103	                mb = int(s.opts.MaxPending)
  5104	        }
  5105	        // Track what we have sent.
  5106	        var sentBytes int
  5107	
  5108	        // Loop over batch, which defaults to 1.
  5109	        for i := 0; i < batch; i++ {
  5110	                var (
  5111	                        svp StoreMsg
  5112	                        sm  *StoreMsg
  5113	                        err error
  5114	                )
  5115	                if seq > 0 && req.NextFor == _EMPTY_ {
  5116	                        // Only do direct lookup for first in a batch.
  5117	                        if i == 0 {
  5118	                                sm, err = store.LoadMsg(seq, &svp)
  5119	                        } else {
  5120	                                // We want to use load next with fwcs to step over deleted msgs.
  5121	                                sm, seq, err = store.LoadNextMsg(fwcs, true, seq, &svp)
  5122	                        }
  5123	                        // Bump for next loop if applicable.
  5124	                        seq++
  5125	                } else if req.NextFor != _EMPTY_ {
  5126	                        sm, seq, err = store.LoadNextMsg(req.NextFor, wc, seq, &svp)
  5127	                        seq++
  5128	                } else {
  5129	                        // Batch is not applicable here, this is checked before we get here.
  5130	                        sm, err = store.LoadLastMsg(req.LastFor, &svp)
  5131	                }
  5132	                if err != nil {
  5133	                        // For batches, if we stop early we want to do EOB logic below.
  5134	                        if batch > 1 && i > 0 {
  5135	                                break
  5136	                        }
  5137	                        hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 404 Message Not Found\r\n\r\n")
  5138	                        mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  5139	                        return
  5140	                }
  5141	
  5142	                ts := time.Unix(0, sm.ts).UTC()
  5143	                var hdr []byte
  5144	                if !req.NoHeaders {
  5145	                        hdr = sm.hdr
  5146	                        if isBatchRequest {
  5147	                                if len(hdr) == 0 {
  5148	                                        hdr = fmt.Appendf(nil, dgb, name, sm.subj, sm.seq, ts.Format(time.RFC3339Nano), np, lseq)
  5149	                                } else {
  5150	                                        hdr = copyBytes(hdr)

  4955	func (mset *stream) getDirectMulti(req *JSApiMsgGetRequest, reply string) {
  4956	        // TODO(dlc) - Make configurable?
  4957	        const maxAllowedResponses = 1024
  4958	
  4959	        // We hold the lock here to try to avoid changes out from underneath of us.
  4960	        mset.mu.RLock()
  4961	        defer mset.mu.RUnlock()
  4962	        // Grab store and name.
  4963	        store, name, s := mset.store, mset.cfg.Name, mset.srv
  4964	
  4965	        // Grab MaxBytes
  4966	        mb := req.MaxBytes
  4967	        if mb == 0 && s != nil {
  4968	                // Fill in with the server's MaxPending.
  4969	                mb = int(s.opts.MaxPending)
  4970	        }
  4971	
  4972	        upToSeq := req.UpToSeq
  4973	        // If we have UpToTime set get the proper sequence.
  4974	        if req.UpToTime != nil {
  4975	                upToSeq = store.GetSeqFromTime((*req.UpToTime).UTC())
  4976	                // Avoid selecting a first sequence that will take us to before the stream first
  4977	                // sequence, otherwise we can return messages after the supplied UpToTime.
  4978	                if upToSeq <= mset.state().FirstSeq {
  4979	                        hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 404 No Results\r\n\r\n")
  4980	                        mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4981	                        return
  4982	                }
  4983	                // We need to back off one since this is used to determine start sequence normally,
  4984	                // whereas here we want it to be the ceiling.
  4985	                upToSeq--
  4986	        }
  4987	        // If not set, set to the last sequence and remember that for EOB.
  4988	        if upToSeq == 0 {
  4989	                var state StreamState
  4990	                mset.store.FastState(&state)
  4991	                upToSeq = state.LastSeq
  4992	        }
  4993	
  4994	        seqs, err := store.MultiLastSeqs(req.MultiLastFor, upToSeq, maxAllowedResponses)
  4995	        if err != nil {
  4996	                var hdr []byte
  4997	                if err == ErrTooManyResults {
  4998	                        hdr = []byte("NATS/1.0 413 Too Many Results\r\n\r\n")
  4999	                } else {
  5000	                        hdr = []byte(fmt.Sprintf("NATS/1.0 500 %v\r\n\r\n", err))

  1359	// subjectsOverlap to see if these subjects overlap with existing subjects.
  1360	// Use only for non-clustered JetStream
  1361	// RLock minimum should be held.
  1362	func (jsa *jsAccount) subjectsOverlap(subjects []string, self *stream) bool {
  1363	        for _, mset := range jsa.streams {
  1364	                if self != nil && mset == self {
  1365	                        continue
  1366	                }
  1367	                for _, subj := range mset.cfg.Subjects {
  1368	                        for _, tsubj := range subjects {
  1369	                                if SubjectsCollide(tsubj, subj) {
  1370	                                        return true
  1371	                                }
  1372	                        }
  1373	                }
  1374	        }
  1375	        return false
  1376	}
